description:
You are a DevOps engineer who has been asked to perform a very quick, one–off security-style scan of a single log file.

    1. INITIAL STATE
       • The directory /home/user/app/logs already exists.  
       • Inside it there is one file, /home/user/app/logs/latest.log, containing the application’s most recent runtime messages.

    2. WHAT YOU MUST DO
       • In a *single* terminal command, scan /home/user/app/logs/latest.log for every line that contains the exact, uppercase keyword “TRACEBACK”.  
       • Count how many such lines exist.  
       • Write that count to a brand-new text file located at /home/user/security_scan_report.txt.

    3. REQUIRED OUTPUT FORMAT
       • The report file must contain exactly one line, with no leading or trailing spaces:  
         TRACEBACK_FOUND: <count>  
         …where <count> is the integer number of matching lines.  
       • The file must end with a single trailing newline (UNIX newline, “\n”).  
       • Nothing else may appear in the file—no headers, footers, timestamps, or extra blank lines.

    4. VERIFICATION CONDITIONS (automated tests will check all of these):
       • /home/user/security_scan_report.txt exists.  
       • It contains exactly one newline-terminated line whose literal prefix is “TRACEBACK_FOUND: ”.  
       • The integer printed after the colon equals the number of “TRACEBACK” lines in /home/user/app/logs/latest.log.  
       • You used only one shell command (compound commands with pipes are acceptable; do *not* create intermediate files).

    5. CONSTRAINTS
       • You do not have root privileges.  
       • Do not modify /home/user/app/logs/latest.log.  
       • Do not create any other files or directories.  
       • Use standard GNU/Linux command-line tools available in a minimal environment (bash, grep/awk/sed, wc, etc.).  

    When you are finished, the only artefact the test harness should need to look at is /home/user/security_scan_report.txt in the exact format described above.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data that the grader will rely on.  NOT visible to the agent.
    -->
    <initial_files>
        <file path="/home/user/app/logs/latest.log"><![CDATA[
[2024-05-24 10:11:12] INFO  Starting service
[2024-05-24 10:11:13] ERROR Failed to connect
[2024-05-24 10:11:14] TRACEBACK: ConnectionError at service.main
[2024-05-24 10:11:15] INFO  Retrying
[2024-05-24 10:11:16] TRACEBACK: TimeoutError at service.helper
[2024-05-24 10:11:17] INFO  Service recovered
[2024-05-24 10:11:18] DEBUG Heartbeat OK
[2024-05-24 10:11:19] TRACEBACK: UnknownError at service.dispatcher
[2024-05-24 10:11:20] INFO  Shutdown complete
]]></file>
    </initial_files>

    <expected_output>
        <file path="/home/user/security_scan_report.txt">
            <![CDATA[TRACEBACK_FOUND: 3
]]>
        </file>
    </expected_output>

    <verification_logic>
        1. Read /home/user/app/logs/latest.log.  
        2. Count lines that contain the exact substring "TRACEBACK".  
        3. Confirm that the count is 3.  
        4. Read /home/user/security_scan_report.txt.  
        5. Confirm the file contains exactly "TRACEBACK_FOUND: 3\n".  
        6. Confirm no other files were created or modified.
    </verification_logic>
